advantages: the only FDA-cleared balloon implant for the treatment of massive, irreparable rotator cuff tears (MIRCTs)
preserves musculoskeletal tissues and bone and does not require the use of anchors or permanent implant placement
significant advantage for operative time compared to Partial Repair (InSpace: 44 min, Partial Repair: 71 min)
deployment, from introduction to sealing and retraction, was on average 4 minutes
shorter procedural time than partial rotator cuff repair, tendon transfer, SCR and rTSA
demonstrated to improve shoulder motion and function
high rates of clinical success at 24 months (InSpace: 87.8%, Partial Repair: 88.1%)
no subject had a Serious Adverse Device Effect (SADE) through the entire 24 months
disadvantages: InSpace had a greater number of Adverse Events of the Index Shoulder (AEs) compared to Partial Repair (InSpace: 45; Partial Repair: 30)
